title: Slow Cooker Green Bean Casserole
description: Creamy, cheesy green bean casserole made in the slow cooker — frees up oven space for holidays and gatherings. Easy to make ahead.

ingredients.name: fresh green beans, trimmed and cut into bite-sized pieces (or 2 cans, drained)
fried onions, plus extra for topping

instructions.text: Lightly grease the inside of the slow cooker with non-stick spray or butter.
In a large bowl, mix together the cream of mushroom soup, milk, cheddar cheese, sour cream, garlic powder, onion powder, and black pepper until smooth.
Place green beans (and mushrooms if using) in the slow cooker. Pour the soup mixture over and stir to coat evenly. Sprinkle 1 cup of fried onions over the top.
Cover and cook on Low for 4–5 hours or High for 2–3 hours, until green beans are tender.
Sprinkle extra fried onions over the top. Cover and cook an additional 15–20 minutes to heat the topping. Stir lightly before serving.

notes:
If using fresh green beans, blanch them briefly before adding to the slow cooker for better texture.

Serves 8–10 as a side dish at a holiday meal with multiple sides. Halves easily for a smaller crowd.

A good make-ahead option — assemble everything except the final onion topping, refrigerate overnight, and start the slow cooker the next day.
